Christian Religious Studies.q: According to the Genesis account of creation, what did God create on the very first day?
o: Light to separate day from night

Christian Religious Studies.q: On which day of creation did God create man in His own image and likeness?
What specific responsibility did God assign to man in the Garden of Eden?
o: To build high towers and cities
To cultivate, guard, and keep the garden
To hunt wild beasts for sacrifice
To name the stars in the sky

Christian Religious Studies.q: The primary consequence of Adam and Eve's disobedience to God's command in Eden was ________.
o: Separation from God's presence and spiritual death
The destruction of the garden by fire

Christian Religious Studies.q: Who was the first son of Adam and Eve who killed his brother Abel out of jealousy?
God instructed Noah to build a wooden ark because He intended to destroy the wicked world using ________.
o: A massive global flood of water

Christian Religious Studies.q: What did God use as a divine sign of His covenant with Noah never to destroy the earth with water again?
What was Abraham's original native homeland before God called him to journey to an unknown country?
How old was Abraham when God explicitly called him to leave his father's house?
God promised Abraham all the following blessings in His covenant EXCEPT ________.
o: Making him a father of many great nations
Giving the land of Canaan to his descendants
Making him the ultimate king of Egypt
Blessing all families of the earth through him

Christian Religious Studies.q: Who was Abraham's wife who miraculously gave birth to Isaac in her extreme old age?
God tested Abraham's supreme faith and total obedience by commanding him to ________.
o: Build a massive stone tower in Haran
Offer his only son Isaac as a burnt sacrifice
Send his nephew Lot away to Sodom
Fast for forty consecutive days and nights
Wage war against the Philistine armies

Christian Religious Studies.q: Who were the twin sons born to Isaac and his wife Rebekah?
Esau foolishly traded away his highly valuable legal birthright to his brother Jacob in exchange for ________.
o: A portion of red lentil stew
A beautiful coat of many colors
A plot of fertile farming land

Christian Religious Studies.q: Jacob's name was divinely changed to 'Israel' after he experienced a night-long spiritual wrestling encounter at ________.
Why did Joseph's older brothers hate him intensely and plot to eliminate him?
o: Because he stole their flock of sheep
Because of his prophetic dreams and their father's favoritism
Because he refused to work on the family farm
Because he was older and more arrogant
Because he destroyed their family altars

Christian Religious Studies.q: Joseph rose from being an Egyptian prison inmate to become the Prime Minister of Egypt because he could ________.
o: Build massive crop storage pyramids rapidly
Perform striking physical miracles with a staff

Christian Religious Studies.q: In which specific land within Egypt did Jacob and his sons settle during the severe global famine?
God uniquely appeared to Moses and called him to liberate Israel while he was tending sheep near Mount ________.
What miraculous sign did God use to catch Moses' attention before speaking to him directly?
o: A bush that burned with fire but was not consumed
A loud thunderclap from a clear sky
A river that instantly turned into blood
A massive flock of white doves landing

Christian Religious Studies.q: What was the tenth and final plague that forced Pharaoh to release the Israelites from bondage?
o: A dense swarm of locusts over fields
The complete death of all firstborn humans and animals in Egypt
Three days of total pitch darkness
A severe outbreak of painful bodily boils
Turning all water sources into thick blood

Christian Religious Studies.q: During the historic Exodus, God safely led the Israelites through the desert using a ________.
o: Pillar of cloud by day and a pillar of fire by night
Continuous sound of a trumpet blowing

Christian Religious Studies.q: Moses miraculously parted the waters of the ________ to allow Israel to escape Pharaoh's charging armies.
God provisioned the hungry Israelites with sweet bread-like food from heaven during their desert journey called ________.
On which mountain did God formally deliver the Ten Commandments tablets to Moses?
Who was Moses' loyal assistant and successor who safely led the Israelites across the River Jordan into Canaan?
The formidable walls of which Canaanite city collapsed completely after Israel marched around them for seven days?
Who was the notable female prophet and judge in Israel who inspired Barak to defeat Sisera's Canaanite forces?
God miraculously reduced Gideon's large army to just ________ men to demonstrate that victory belongs to God alone.
Who was the strong Israelite judge whose immense physical strength lay in his uncut Nazirite hair?
Who was the pious child prophet who was dedicated to God's service at Shiloh and raised by High Priest Eli?
Who was formally anointed by Prophet Samuel as the very first human king over Israel?
King Saul was ultimately rejected by God from reigning over Israel because of his ________.
o: Disobedience to God's explicit commands regarding sacrifices
Failure to build a stone temple

Christian Religious Studies.q: David, the shepherd boy, defeated the towering Philistine giant champion Goliath using a ________.
Which king of Israel was blessed with unmatched wisdom and built the magnificent First Temple for God in Jerusalem?
Prophet Elijah staged a dramatic spiritual contest against the 450 false prophets of Baal at Mount ________.
Which prophet was thrown into a den of hungry lions because he refused to stop praying to the Almighty God?
Which prophet was swallowed by a massive fish after attempting to flee from God's command to preach in Nineveh?
In which historical Judean town was Jesus Christ born, fulfilling ancient prophetic scriptures?
Which angel formally announced the miraculous birth of Jesus to the Virgin Mary?
Who was the prophetic forerunner of Jesus who baptized Him in the waters of the River Jordan?
Immediately after His baptism, Jesus was led by the Holy Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil for ________.
What was the very first public miracle performed by Jesus, as recorded in the Gospel of John at a wedding feast?
o: Healing a lame man at Bethesda
Turning water into high-quality wine at Cana
Calming a violent sea storm with a word

Christian Religious Studies.q: How many core disciples did Jesus select to form His inner circle of apostles for ministry?
Which disciple was a tax collector before being called by Jesus to become a fisher of men?
Jesus utilized simple, short earthly stories with deep heavenly meanings to teach crowds. These stories are called ________.
In the Parable of the Prodigal Son, what was the father's reaction when his repentant son returned home?
o: He locked him out of the family compound
He welcomed him with immense joy, compassion, and a great feast
He made him work as a slave to repay lost funds
He disowned him permanently before elders
He sent him to a distant city for correction

Christian Religious Studies.q: Which parable did Jesus share specifically to teach the ultimate lesson of loving and helping our neighbors unconditionally?
Jesus demonstrated His supreme power over physical death by raising His friend ________ from the dead after four days in a tomb.
During the Last Supper with His disciples, the bread and wine shared by Jesus explicitly represented His ________.
o: Body and blood given for the remission of sins

Christian Religious Studies.q: In which garden did Jesus experience deep agonizing prayer and distress before His arrest?
Which disciple deceptively betrayed Jesus into the hands of the chief priests for thirty pieces of silver?
Who was the Roman Governor who presided over the judicial trial of Jesus and reluctantly sentenced Him to crucifixion?
The specific hill site outside Jerusalem where Jesus Christ was crucified is known as ________.
On which day following His crucifixion and burial did Jesus triumphantly rise from the dead?
The monumental event where Jesus was taken up into heaven in a cloud before the eyes of His apostles is the ________.
The spectacular descent of the Holy Spirit upon the gathered disciples, marked by tongues of fire, occurred on the Day of ________.
Who was the first Christian martyr who was stoned to death for boldly preaching the Gospel message?
What was the original name of the zealous Pharisee who persecuted early Christians before his miraculous conversion on the road to Damascus?
According to Paul's epistle to the Galatians, all the following are attributes of the Fruit of the Spirit EXCEPT ________.
